metadata.summary: Specify step and flyscan paths in a serializable, efficient and Pythonic way

documentation: [![CI](https://github.com/bluesky/scanspec/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/bluesky/scanspec/actions/workflows/ci.yml) [![Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/bluesky/scanspec/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/bluesky/scanspec) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/scanspec.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/scanspec) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) # scanspec Specify step and flyscan paths in a serializable, efficient and Pythonic way using combinations of: - Specs like Line or Spiral - Optionally Snaking - Zip, Product and Concat to compose - Masks with multiple Regions to restrict Serialize the Spec rather than the expanded Path and reconstruct it on the server. It can them be iterated over like a [cycler][], or a stack of scan Frames can be produced and expanded Paths created to consume chunk by chunk. [cycler]: https://matplotlib.org/cycler/ Source | :---: | :---: PyPI | `pip install scanspec` Docker | `docker run ghcr.io/bluesky/scanspec:latest` Documentation | Releases | An example ScanSpec of a 2D snaked grid flyscan inside a circle spending 0.4s at each point: ```python from scanspec.specs import Line, fly from scanspec.regions import Circle grid = Line(y, 2.1, 3.8, 12) * ~Line(x, 0.5, 1.5, 10) spec = fly(grid, 0.4) & Circle(x, y, 1.0, 2.8, radius=0.5) ``` Which when plotted looks like: ![plot][] Scan points can be iterated through directly for convenience: ```python for point in spec.midpoints(): print(point) # ... # {'y': 3.1818181818181817, 'x': 0.8333333333333333, 'DURATION': 0.4} # {'y': 3.1818181818181817, 'x': 0.7222222222222222, 'DURATION': 0.4} ``` or a Path created from the stack of Frames and chunks of a given length consumed from it for performance: ```python from scanspec.core import Path stack = spec.calculate() len(stack[0]) # 44 stack[0].axes() # ['y', 'x', 'DURATION'] path = Path(stack, start=5, num=30) chunk = path.consume(10) chunk.midpoints # {'x': , 'y': , 'DURATION': } chunk.upper # bounds are same dimensionality as positions ``` See https://bluesky.github.io/scanspec for more detailed documentation. [plot]: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bluesky/scanspec/master/docs/images/plot_spec.png

modules.name: scanspec
documentation:
Top level API.

```
.. data:: __version__
    :type: str

    Version number as calculated by https://github.com/pypa/setuptools_scm
```

modules.name: scanspec.service
documentation: FastAPI service to query information about Specs.

functions.name: scanspec.service.valid
documentation:
Validate wether a ScanSpec[str] can produce a viable scan.

Args:
    spec: The scanspec to validate

Returns:
    ValidResponse: A canonical version of the spec if it is valid.
        An error otherwise.

functions.name: scanspec.service.midpoints
documentation:
Generate midpoints from a scanspec.

A scanspec can produce bounded points (i.e. a point is valid if an
axis is between a minimum and and a maximum, see /bounds). The midpoints
are the middle of each set of bounds.

Args:
    request: Scanspec and formatting info.

Returns:
    MidpointsResponse: Midpoints of the scan

functions.name: scanspec.service.bounds
documentation:
Generate bounds from a scanspec.

A scanspec can produce points with lower and upper bounds.

Args:
    request: Scanspec and formatting info.

Returns:
    BoundsResponse: Bounds of the scan

functions.name: scanspec.service.gap
documentation:
Generate gaps from a scanspec.

A scanspec may indicate if there is a gap between two frames.
The array returned corresponds to whether or not there is a gap
after each frame.

Args:
    spec: Scanspec and formatting info.

Returns:
    GapResponse: Bounds of the scan

functions.name: scanspec.service.smallest_step
documentation:
Calculate the smallest step in a scan, both absolutely and per-axis.

Ignore any steps of size 0.

Args:
    spec: The spec of the scan

Returns:
    SmallestStepResponse: A description of the smallest steps in the spec

functions.name: scanspec.service._to_chunk
documentation: Utility Functions

functions.name: scanspec.service._format_axes_points
documentation:
Convert points to a requested format.

Args:
    axes_points: The points to convert
    format: The target format

Raises:
    KeyError: If the function does not support the given format

Returns:
    Mapping[str, Points]: A mapping of axis to formatted points.

functions.name: scanspec.service._reduce_frames
documentation:
Removes frames from a spec so len(path) < max_frames.

Args:
    stack: A stack of Frames created by a spec
    max_frames: The maximum number of frames the user wishes to be returned

functions.name: scanspec.service._sub_sample
documentation:
Provides a sub-sample Frames object whilst preserving its core structure.

Args:
    frames: the Frames object to be reduced
    ratio: the reduction ratio of the dimension

functions.name: scanspec.service._abs_diffs
documentation:
Calculates the absolute differences between adjacent elements in the array.

Args:
    array: A 1xN array of numerical values

Returns:
    A newly constucted array of absolute differences

functions.name: scanspec.service.run_app
documentation: Run an application providing the scanspec service.

functions.name: scanspec.service.scanspec_schema_text
documentation:
Generate the OpenAPI schema for the service as a string.

Returns:
    str: The OpenAPI schema

classes.name: scanspec.service.ValidResponse
documentation: Response model for spec validation.

classes.name: scanspec.service.PointsFormat
documentation: Formats in which we can return points.

classes.name: scanspec.service.PointsRequest
documentation: A request for generated scan points.

classes.name: scanspec.service.GeneratedPointsResponse
documentation: Base class for responses that include generated point data.

classes.name: scanspec.service.MidpointsResponse
documentation: Midpoints of a generated scan.

classes.name: scanspec.service.BoundsResponse
documentation: Bounds of a generated scan.

classes.name: scanspec.service.GapResponse
documentation: Presence of gaps in a generated scan.

classes.name: scanspec.service.SmallestStepResponse

modules.name: scanspec.sphinxext
documentation: An example_spec directive.

documentation:
Force matplotlib PlotDirective to always create figures.

This is needed even if source rst hasn't changed, as we often use
example_spec from within docstrings

documentation: Setup this extension in sphinx.

Runs `plot_spec` on the `spec` definied in the content.

modules.name: scanspec.__main__
documentation: Interface for `python -m scanspec`.

modules.name: scanspec.core
documentation: Core classes like `Frames` and `Path`.

functions.name: scanspec.core.discriminated_union_of_subclasses
scanspec.core.if_instance_do
documentation:
If x is of type cls then return func(x), otherwise return NotImplemented.

Used as a helper when implementing operator overloading.

functions.name: scanspec.core.gap_between_frames
documentation: Is there a gap between end of frames1 and start of frames2.

functions.name: scanspec.core.squash_frames
documentation:
Squash a stack of nested Frames into a single one.

Args:

: stack: The Frames stack to squash, from slowest to fastest moving
  check_path_changes: If True then check that nesting the output

  > Frames object within others will provide the same path
  > as nesting the input Frames stack within others

See Also:

: `why-squash-can-change-path`

```pycon
>>> fx = SnakedFrames({"x": np.array([1, 2])})
>>> fy = Frames({"y": np.array([3, 4])})
>>> squash_frames([fy, fx]).midpoints
{'y': array([3, 3, 4, 4]), 'x': array([1, 2, 2, 1])}
```

classes.name: scanspec.core.Frames

methods.name: scanspec.core.Frames.axes
documentation:
The axes which will move during the scan.

These will be present in `midpoints`, `lower` and `upper`.

methods.name: scanspec.core.Frames.__len__
documentation: The number of frames in this section of the scan.

methods.name: scanspec.core.Frames.extract
documentation:
Return a new Frames object restricted to the indices provided.

Args:

: indices: The indices of the frames to extract, modulo scan length
  calculate_gap: If True then recalculate the gap from upper and lower

```pycon
>>> frames = Frames({"x": np.array([1, 2, 3])})
>>> frames.extract(np.array([1, 0, 1])).midpoints
{'x': array([2, 1, 2])}
```

methods.name: scanspec.core.Frames.concat
documentation:
Return a new Frames object concatenating self and other.

Requires both Frames objects to have the same axes, but not necessarily in
the same order. The order is inherited from self, so other may be reordered.

Args:

: other: The Frames to concatenate to self
  gap: Whether to force a gap between the two Frames objects

```pycon
>>> frames = Frames({"x": np.array([1, 2, 3]), "y": np.array([6, 5, 4])})
>>> frames2 = Frames({"y": np.array([3, 2, 1]), "x": np.array([4, 5, 6])})
>>> frames.concat(frames2).midpoints
{'x': array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]), 'y': array([6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1])}
```

methods.name: scanspec.core.Frames.zip
documentation:
Return a new Frames object merging self and other.

Require both Frames objects to not share axes.

```pycon
>>> fx = Frames({"x": np.array([1, 2, 3])})
>>> fy = Frames({"y": np.array([5, 6, 7])})
>>> fx.zip(fy).midpoints
{'x': array([1, 2, 3]), 'y': array([5, 6, 7])}
```

Represents a series of scan frames along a number of axes.

During a scan each axis will traverse lower-midpoint-upper for each frame.

Args:
    midpoints: The midpoints of scan frames for each axis
    lower: Lower bounds of scan frames if different from midpoints
    upper: Upper bounds of scan frames if different from midpoints
    gap: If supplied, define if there is a gap between frame and previous
        otherwise it is calculated by looking at lower and upper bounds

Typically used in two ways:

- A list of Frames objects returned from `Spec.calculate` represents a scan
  as a linear stack of frames. Interpreted as nested from slowest moving to
  fastest moving, so each faster Frames object will iterate once per
  position of the slower Frames object. It is passed to a `Path` for
  calculation of the actual scan path.
- A single Frames object returned from `Path.consume` represents a chunk of
  frames forming part of a scan path, for interpretation by the code
  that will actually perform the scan.

See Also:
    `technical-terms`

classes.name: scanspec.core.SnakedFrames

methods.name: scanspec.core.SnakedFrames.from_frames
documentation: Create a snaked version of a `Frames` object.

methods.name: scanspec.core.SnakedFrames.extract
documentation:
Return a new Frames object restricted to the indices provided.

Args:

: indices: The indices of the frames to extract, can extend past len(self)
  calculate_gap: If True then recalculate the gap from upper and lower

```pycon
>>> frames = SnakedFrames({"x": np.array([1, 2, 3])})
>>> frames.extract(np.array([0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5])).midpoints
{'x': array([1, 2, 3, 3, 2, 1])}
```
Like a `Frames` object, but each alternate repetition will run in reverse.

classes.name: scanspec.core.Path

methods.name: scanspec.core.Path.consume
documentation:
Consume at most num frames from the Path and return as a Frames object.

```pycon
>>> fx = SnakedFrames({"x": np.array([1, 2])})
>>> fy = Frames({"y": np.array([3, 4])})
>>> path = Path([fy, fx])
>>> path.consume(3).midpoints
{'y': array([3, 3, 4]), 'x': array([1, 2, 2])}
>>> path.consume(3).midpoints
{'y': array([4]), 'x': array([1])}
>>> path.consume(3).midpoints
{'y': array([], dtype=int64), 'x': array([], dtype=int64)}
```

methods.name: scanspec.core.Path.__len__
documentation: Number of frames left in a scan, reduces when `consume` is called.

A consumable route through a stack of Frames, representing a scan path.

Args:
    stack: The Frames stack describing the scan, from slowest to fastest
        moving
    start: The index of where in the Path to start
    num: The number of scan frames to produce after start. None means up to
        the end

See Also:
    `iterate-a-spec`

classes.name: scanspec.core.Midpoints

methods.name: scanspec.core.Midpoints.axes
documentation: The axes that will be present in each points dictionary.

methods.name: scanspec.core.Midpoints.__len__
documentation: The number of dictionaries that will be produced if iterated over.

methods.name: scanspec.core.Midpoints.__iter__
documentation: Yield {axis: midpoint} for each frame in the scan.

Convenience iterable that produces the scan midpoints for each axis.

For better performance, consume from a `Path` instead.

Args:
: stack: The stack of Frames describing the scan, from slowest to fastest

  : moving

See Also:

: `iterate-a-spec`

```pycon
>>> fx = SnakedFrames({"x": np.array([1, 2])})
>>> fy = Frames({"y": np.array([3, 4])})
>>> mp = Midpoints([fy, fx])
>>> for p in mp: print(p)
{'y': np.int64(3), 'x': np.int64(1)}
{'y': np.int64(3), 'x': np.int64(2)}
{'y': np.int64(4), 'x': np.int64(2)}
{'y': np.int64(4), 'x': np.int64(1)}
```

modules.name: scanspec.specs
documentation:
`Spec` and its subclasses.

```
.. inheritance-diagram:: scanspec.specs
    :top-classes: scanspec.specs.Spec
    :parts: 1
```

functions.name: scanspec.specs.fly
documentation:
Flyscan, zipping with fixed duration for every frame.

Args:

: spec: The source `Spec` to continuously move
  duration: How long to spend at each frame in the spec

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line, fly

    spec = fly(Line("x", 1, 2, 3), 0.1)
```

functions.name: scanspec.specs.step
documentation:
Step scan, with num frames of given duration at each frame in the spec.

Args:

: spec: The source `Spec` with midpoints to move to and stop
  duration: The duration of each scan frame
  num: Number of frames to produce with given duration at each of frame

  > in the spec

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line, step

    spec = step(Line("x", 1, 2, 3), 0.1)
```

functions.name: scanspec.specs.get_constant_duration
documentation:
Returns the duration of a number of ScanSpec frames, if known and consistent.

Args:
    frames (List[Frames]): A number of Frame objects

Returns:
    duration (float): if all frames have a consistent duration
    None: otherwise

variables.name: scanspec.specs.Line.bounded
documentation: type:ignore

variables.name: scanspec.specs.Static.duration
documentation: type:ignore

variables.name: scanspec.specs.Spiral.spaced
documentation: type:ignore

classes.name: scanspec.specs.Spec

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Spec.axes
documentation:
Return the list of axes that are present in the scan.

Ordered from slowest moving to fastest moving.

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Spec.calculate
documentation:
Produce a stack of nested `Frames` that form the scan.

Ordered from slowest moving to fastest moving.

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Spec.frames
documentation: Expand all the scan `Frames` and return them.

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Spec.midpoints
documentation: Return `Midpoints` that can be iterated point by point.

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Spec.shape
documentation: Return the final, simplified shape of the scan.

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Spec.zip
documentation: `Zip` the Spec with another, iterating in tandem.

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Spec.concat
documentation: `Concat` the Spec with another, iterating one after the other.

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Spec.serialize
documentation: Serialize the Spec to a dictionary.

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Spec.deserialize
documentation: Deserialize a Spec from a dictionary.

A serializable representation of the type and parameters of a scan.

Abstract baseclass for the specification of a scan. Supports operators:

- `*`: Outer `Product` of two Specs, nesting the second within the first.
  If the first operand is an integer, wrap it in a `Repeat`
- `&`: `Mask` the Spec with a `Region`, excluding midpoints outside of it
- `~`: `Snake` the Spec, reversing every other iteration of it

classes.name: scanspec.specs.Product
documentation:
Outer product of two Specs, nesting inner within outer.

This means that inner will run in its entirety at each point in outer.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line

    spec = Line("y", 1, 2, 3) * Line("x", 3, 4, 12)
```

classes.name: scanspec.specs.Repeat
documentation:
Repeat an empty frame num times.

Can be used on the outside of a scan to repeat the same scan many times.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line

    spec = 2 * ~Line.bounded("x", 3, 4, 1)
```

If you want snaked axes to have no gap between iterations you can do:

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line, Repeat

    spec = Repeat(2, gap=False) * ~Line.bounded("x", 3, 4, 1)
```

:::{note}
There is no turnaround arrow at x=4
:::

classes.name: scanspec.specs.Zip
documentation:
Run two Specs in parallel, merging their midpoints together.

Typically formed using `Spec.zip`.

Stacks of Frames are merged by:

- If right creates a stack of a single Frames object of size 1, expand it to
  the size of the fastest Frames object created by left
- Merge individual Frames objects together from fastest to slowest

This means that Zipping a Spec producing stack [l2, l1] with a Spec
producing stack [r1] will assert len(l1)==len(r1), and produce
stack [l2, l1.zip(r1)].

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line

    spec = Line("z", 1, 2, 3) * Line("y", 3, 4, 5).zip(Line("x", 4, 5, 5))
```

classes.name: scanspec.specs.Mask

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Mask.__or__
documentation: *+ bind more tightly than &|^ so without these overrides we
would need to add brackets to all combinations of Regions

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Mask.__sub__
documentation: This is here for completeness, tends not to be called as - binds
tighter than &

Restrict Spec to only midpoints that fall inside the given Region.

Typically created with the `&` operator. It also pushes down the
`& | ^ -` operators to its `Region` to avoid the need for brackets on
combinations of Regions.

If a Region spans multiple Frames objects, they will be squashed together.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.regions import Circle
    from scanspec.specs import Line

    spec = Line("y", 1, 3, 3) * Line("x", 3, 5, 5) & Circle("x", "y", 4, 2, 1.2)
```

See Also: `why-squash-can-change-path`

classes.name: scanspec.specs.Snake
documentation:
Run the Spec in reverse on every other iteration when nested.

Typically created with the `~` operator.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line

    spec = Line("y", 1, 3, 3) * ~Line("x", 3, 5, 5)
```

classes.name: scanspec.specs.Concat
documentation:
Concatenate two Specs together, running one after the other.

Each Dimension of left and right must contain the same axes. Typically
formed using `Spec.concat`.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line

    spec = Line("x", 1, 3, 3).concat(Line("x", 4, 5, 5))
```

classes.name: scanspec.specs.Squash
documentation:
Squash a stack of Frames together into a single expanded Frames object.

See Also:

: `why-squash-can-change-path`

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line, Squash

    spec = Squash(Line("y", 1, 2, 3) * Line("x", 0, 1, 4))
```

classes.name: scanspec.specs.Line

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Line.bounded
documentation:
Specify a Line by extreme bounds instead of midpoints.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line

    spec = Line.bounded("x", 1, 2, 5)
```

Linearly spaced frames with start and stop as first and last midpoints.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line

    spec = Line("x", 1, 2, 5)
```

classes.name: scanspec.specs.Static

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Static.duration
documentation:
A static spec with no motion, only a duration repeated "num" times.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line, Static

    spec = Line("y", 1, 2, 3).zip(Static.duration(0.1))
```

A static frame, repeated num times, with axis at value.

Can be used to set axis=value at every point in a scan.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line, Static

    spec = Line("y", 1, 2, 3).zip(Static("x", 3))
```

classes.name: scanspec.specs.Spiral

methods.name: scanspec.specs.Spiral.spaced
documentation:
Specify a Spiral equally spaced in "x_axis" and "y_axis".

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Spiral

    spec = Spiral.spaced("x", "y", 0, 0, 10, 3)
```

Archimedean spiral of "x_axis" and "y_axis".

Starts at centre point ("x_start", "y_start") with angle "rotate". Produces
"num" points in a spiral spanning width of "x_range" and height of "y_range"

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Spiral

    spec = Spiral("x", "y", 1, 5, 10, 50, 30)
```

modules.name: scanspec.regions
documentation:
`Region` and its subclasses.

```
.. inheritance-diagram:: scanspec.regions
    :top-classes: scanspec.regions.Region
    :parts: 1
```

functions.name: scanspec.regions.get_mask
documentation:
Return a mask of the points inside the region.

If there is an overlap of axes of region and points return a
mask of the points in the region, otherwise return all ones

functions.name: scanspec.regions.find_regions
documentation: Recursively yield Regions from obj and its children.

classes.name: scanspec.regions.Region

methods.name: scanspec.regions.Region.axis_sets
documentation: Produce the non-overlapping sets of axes this region spans.

methods.name: scanspec.regions.Region.mask
documentation: Produce a mask of which points are in the region.

methods.name: scanspec.regions.Region.serialize
documentation: Serialize the Region to a dictionary.

methods.name: scanspec.regions.Region.deserialize
documentation: Deserialize a Region from a dictionary.

Abstract baseclass for a Region that can `Mask` a `Spec`.

Supports operators:

- `|`: `UnionOf` two Regions, midpoints present in either
- `&`: `IntersectionOf` two Regions, midpoints present in both
- `-`: `DifferenceOf` two Regions, midpoints present in first not second
- `^`: `SymmetricDifferenceOf` two Regions, midpoints present in one not both

classes.name: scanspec.regions.CombinationOf
documentation: Abstract baseclass for a combination of two regions, left and right.

classes.name: scanspec.regions.UnionOf
documentation:
A point is in UnionOf(a, b) if in either a or b.

Typically created with the `|` operator

```pycon
>>> r = Range("x", 0.5, 2.5) | Range("x", 1.5, 3.5)
>>> r.mask({"x": np.array([0, 1, 2, 3, 4])})
array([False,  True,  True,  True, False])
```
Naming so we don't clash with typing.Union

classes.name: scanspec.regions.IntersectionOf
documentation:
A point is in IntersectionOf(a, b) if in both a and b.

Typically created with the `&` operator.

```pycon
>>> r = Range("x", 0.5, 2.5) & Range("x", 1.5, 3.5)
>>> r.mask({"x": np.array([0, 1, 2, 3, 4])})
array([False, False,  True, False, False])
```

classes.name: scanspec.regions.DifferenceOf
documentation:
A point is in DifferenceOf(a, b) if in a and not in b.

Typically created with the `-` operator.

```pycon
>>> r = Range("x", 0.5, 2.5) - Range("x", 1.5, 3.5)
>>> r.mask({"x": np.array([0, 1, 2, 3, 4])})
array([False,  True, False, False, False])
```

classes.name: scanspec.regions.SymmetricDifferenceOf
documentation:
A point is in SymmetricDifferenceOf(a, b) if in either a or b, but not both.

Typically created with the `^` operator.

```pycon
>>> r = Range("x", 0.5, 2.5) ^ Range("x", 1.5, 3.5)
>>> r.mask({"x": np.array([0, 1, 2, 3, 4])})
array([False,  True, False,  True, False])
```

classes.name: scanspec.regions.Range
documentation:
Mask contains points of axis >= min and \<= max.

```pycon
>>> r = Range("x", 1, 2)
>>> r.mask({"x": np.array([0, 1, 2, 3, 4])})
array([False,  True,  True, False, False])
```

classes.name: scanspec.regions.Rectangle
documentation:
Mask contains points of axis within a rotated xy rectangle.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.regions import Rectangle
    from scanspec.specs import Line

    grid = Line("y", 1, 3, 10) * ~Line("x", 0, 2, 10)
    spec = grid & Rectangle("x", "y", 0, 1.1, 1.5, 2.1, 30)
```

classes.name: scanspec.regions.Polygon
documentation:
Mask contains points of axis within a rotated xy polygon.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.regions import Polygon
    from scanspec.specs import Line

    grid = Line("y", 3, 8, 10) * ~Line("x", 1 ,8, 10)
    spec = grid & Polygon("x", "y", [1.0, 6.0, 8.0, 2.0], [4.0, 10.0, 6.0, 1.0])
```

classes.name: scanspec.regions.Circle
documentation:
Mask contains points of axis within an xy circle of given radius.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.regions import Circle
    from scanspec.specs import Line

    grid = Line("y", 1, 3, 10) * ~Line("x", 0, 2, 10)
    spec = grid & Circle("x", "y", 1, 2, 0.9)
```

classes.name: scanspec.regions.Ellipse
documentation:
Mask contains points of axis within an xy ellipse of given radius.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.regions import Ellipse
    from scanspec.specs import Line

    grid = Line("y", 3, 8, 10) * ~Line("x", 1 ,8, 10)
    spec = grid & Ellipse("x", "y", 5, 5, 2, 3, 75)
```

modules.name: scanspec.plot
documentation: `plot_spec` to visualize a scan.

functions.name: scanspec.plot.plot_spec
documentation:
Plot a spec, drawing the path taken through the scan.

Uses a different colour for each frame, grey for the turnarounds, and
marks the midpoints with a filled circle if there are less than 200 of
them. If the scan is 2D then 2D regions are shown in black.

```
.. example_spec::

    from scanspec.specs import Line
    from scanspec.regions import Circle

    cube = Line("z", 1, 3, 3) * Line("y", 1, 3, 10) * ~Line("x", 0, 2, 10)
    spec = cube & Circle("x", "y", 1, 2, 0.9)
```

classes.name: scanspec.plot.Arrow3D

methods.name: scanspec.plot.Arrow3D.do_3d_projection
documentation: Added here because of https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/21688
https://stackoverflow.com/a/11156353
